title: wolftpm_rs_shim.c: (void)packet comment contradicts subsequent packet.size use
description: In wolftpm-sys/src/wolftpm_rs_shim.c at line 133, the statement '(void)packet; /* pos / size no longer needed */' claims that packet.size is not needed, but 11 lines later (line 144) the code reads packet.size in the bounds check: 'if (resp_len < TPM2_HEADER_SIZE || resp_len > packet.size)'. The (void) cast in C only suppresses unused-variable warnings; it does not make the variable inaccessible. A future maintainer reading the comment might refactor away packet.size access from line 144, breaking the safety-critical bounds check that prevents out-of-buffer reads. Fix: remove the misleading comment or replace packet.size with sizeof(dev->ctx.cmdBuf) directly to make the bounds check self-contained.
